evidence: api package: 6 files — ExplorationHandlers, HttpApiUtils, PatternSubstitution, RelationUtils, SketchHandlers, WordSketchApiServer. Mixed HTTP handlers and pure-utility helpers.
config package: 4 files — GrammarConfigLoader, PosGroup, RelationConfig, RelationType. Cohesive grammar-config concern.

exploration package: 2 files — SemanticFieldExplorer and AdjectiveCollocateRanker. Comment in AdjectiveCollocateRanker.java says 'Extracted from SemanticFieldExplorer to keep that class below the 400-line threshold', revealing a size-driven split rather than a concern-driven one.

model package: 9 files — AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationResult, ExploreOptions, QueryResults, RelationEdgeType. All are pure data classes; cohesive but the largest package.
query package: 4 files — QueryExecutor (interface), BlackLabQueryExecutor, BlackLabSnippetParser, CollocateQueryHelper. Cohesive around query execution.
utils package: 3 files — CqlUtils, LogDiceCalculator, LongIntHashMap. Cohesive utilities.
viz package: 1 file — RadialPlot only. Thin single-file package.

PatternSubstitution.java in api: imports only pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.utils.CqlUtils and java.util.List — no HTTP dependency. Has no @HttpExchange or server coupling. Only caller is SketchHandlers.
RelationUtils.java in api: a Map-of-aliases constant with one static method; no HTTP dependency. Only called from ExplorationHandlers.

strengths: Packages are small and focused: no package exceeds 9 files, and most have 2-4 files
The model package cleanly isolates all data-transfer objects, preventing DTO coupling from leaking into other layers
The config package correctly owns grammar-loading, POS group definitions, and relation type enum in one place
The indexer/blacklab sub-package correctly separates indexing from querying concerns
The exploration package keeps semantic-field algorithms away from HTTP handling
package_organization.issue_character: Two purely utility classes (PatternSubstitution, RelationUtils) live in the api package despite having no HTTP dependency, and the viz package is an undersized single-file sub-package; these are misclassification issues rather than a structural breakdown.
package_organization.score_rationale: For a 32-file Java project the package structure is well-organised: packages are small, names reflect responsibilities, and cross-cutting concerns (model, config, utils) are cleanly isolated. The main detractors are PatternSubstitution and RelationUtils living in api despite being pure utilities with zero HTTP coupling, and the viz single-file package that creates overhead without providing a meaningful boundary. These are real navigability problems (a developer looking for CQL substitution utilities would not think to look in api) but they are narrow in scope. The codebase scores above average but below 'well-organised with only cosmetic issues'.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution lives in api but has no HTTP dependency and belongs in utils or config
evidence: PatternSubstitution.java imports only CqlUtils (from utils) and java.util.List — no com.sun.net.httpserver or API dependency
The class is a pure CQL-manipulation utility, performing the same concern as RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint
issues.suggestion: Stage 1: move PatternSubstitution to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/utils/PatternSubstitution.java. Stage 2: update the single import in SketchHandlers.java. Validate with: mvn compile. Consider also merging substituteCollocate logic into RelationConfig.getFullPattern(head, collocate) to eliminate the duplication.

issues.summary: RelationUtils lives in api but is a pure alias-resolution utility with no HTTP dependency
evidence: RelationUtils.java imports only java.util.Map — no HTTP or API dependency
Contains only a static RELATION_ALIASES map and a resolveRelationAlias helper method
The alias map maps relation short names to grammar-config IDs — a config/lookup concern, not an HTTP concern
issues.suggestion: Move RelationUtils to the config package (src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/config/RelationUtils.java), since it maps API aliases to grammar-config IDs. Stage 1: rename/move. Stage 2: update import in ExplorationHandlers. Validate with: mvn compile.

issues.summary: The viz package contains a single class (RadialPlot) providing minimal boundary value
evidence: RadialPlot is called only from SketchHandlers.handleVisualRadial in the api package
issues.suggestion: If visualization complexity is not expected to grow, move RadialPlot into the api package (co-located with the handler that uses it) or into utils. Stage 1: move file. Stage 2: update import in SketchHandlers. Validate with: mvn compile.

root_causes: Utility helpers were created in the api package as the API was built and never extracted into utils or config
likely_symptoms: Developers looking for CQL pattern utilities search utils first and miss PatternSubstitution in api
possible_false_positives: AdjectiveCollocateRanker size-split is a pragmatic choice; exploration package is coherent despite the comment
